hero.headline: "Empowering Women Through Awareness & Care"
hero.subtext: A safe, private space to learn, track, and connect. Backed by WHO guidelines and Government of India resources.

learn.title: Menstrual Health Education
learn.subtitle: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), menstrual health is an integral part of sexual and reproductive health and rights.
learn.basics_content: A menstrual cycle typically lasts 21 to 35 days. It is a natural process where the uterus sheds its lining.
learn.hygiene_content: Change your pad or tampon every 4-6 hours to prevent infections. Wash externally with clean water and mild soap.
learn.myths_content: Myth: You shouldn't wash your hair during your period. Fact: Personal hygiene is crucial, and showering/washing hair is perfectly safe.
learn.nutrition_content: Iron-rich foods like spinach and lentils help replenish blood loss. Stay hydrated to reduce cramps.

tracker.title: Private Period Tracker
tracker.subtitle: Track your cycle locally on your device. Your data never leaves your phone.
